title: Name table-driven test cases
scenario: Named cases make failing table-driven tests easier to diagnose and easier for humans to scan.
instructions: Create PercentageCase with name, input, and expected fields, then use it in a table-driven test.

hints.body: Keep the Table-driven domain tests code active and create PercentageCase with name, input, and expected fields, then use it in a table-driven test.
Use the Rust API named in this lesson instead of adding a side helper; the previous arc code should stay in the main solution.
One complete solution for Name table-driven test cases is shown below.

completionExplanation: Name table-driven test cases now extends the active Table-driven domain tests code with percentage named test cases. The previous lesson's behavior remains in the main solution rather than an archived module.
